title: german split peasbauernbrot
description: a rich, cheesy german dish featuring split peas and double-rise-bread, great for the holidays. this keto-friendly recipe is perfect for a winter evening meal.

ingredients.name: sharp cheddar cheese
ingredients.notes: use high-quality, low-carb cheddar for best results.

instructions: in a large mixing bowl, combine warm water and yeast. let sit for 5 minutes, until foamy.
add almond milk, unsalted butter, salt, and 1 cup of split peas to the bowl. mix until a shaggy dough forms.
add remaining 1/2 cup of split peas and mix until dough is sticky but coming together.
knead the dough on a floured surface for 10 minutes, until smooth and elastic.
place dough in a large greased bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let rise in a warm place for 1 hour, or until doubled in size.
punch down dough and shape into a round or oblong loaf. place on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
bake for 35-40 minutes, or until golden brown.
while bread is baking, prepare the cheese filling by combining softened cream cheese, shredded cheddar, and chopped onion in a bowl. mix until smooth.
remove bread from oven and let cool for 5 minutes.
slice the top third off the bread and hollow out the inside, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
fill the bread with the cheese mixture and top with chopped parsley.
place the top slice back on and bake for an additional 5-7 minutes, or until cheese is melted and bubbly.
let cool for 10 minutes before slicing and serving.
increase oven temperature to broil. place bread under broiler for 1-2 minutes, or until top is golden brown and cheese is melted and bubbly.
remove from oven and let cool for a few minutes before slicing and serving.
serve warm, with additional cheese and parsley if desired.
slice into thick wedges and serve immediately, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
